# Test Ad 4: Strong Copy, Weak Research/ICP

**Source:** Crafted to represent polished writing with vague audience targeting
**Expected quality band:** Mixed (~55-70) — strong C, weak R
**Tests:** RMBC imbalance — tests whether severity gating reveals a "Rewrite" on R despite good C

---

## Ad Copy

Subject: The $47 investment that replaced my $4,700/month agency

I fired my marketing agency on a Tuesday.

Not because they were bad people. They showed up to calls, sent reports on time, used all the right buzzwords. But after 8 months and $37,600 in fees, I couldn't point to a single campaign that made us more money than it cost.

The truth hit me at 2am, staring at a spreadsheet that told a story my agency never would: our CAC had tripled. Our ROAS was underwater. And the "brand awareness" they kept celebrating? It hadn't moved the needle on revenue by a single dollar.

So I started looking for something different. Not another agency. Not another course. Something that would actually do the work.

That's when I found AdStack.

AdStack is an AI-powered ad creation platform that writes, designs, and deploys ads across every major channel. But here's what makes it different: it doesn't just generate generic content. It studies your top-performing campaigns, identifies the patterns that actually drive conversions, and reverse-engineers them into new creative that keeps winning.

In my first 30 days, AdStack produced 47 ad variations. Of those, 12 outperformed anything my agency had ever created — one Facebook ad alone generated $23,400 in revenue on a $2,100 spend. That's an 11.1x ROAS.

The kicker? AdStack costs $47/month. My agency was charging $4,700.

I'm not saying every agency is a waste. But if you've ever looked at your marketing spend and thought "there has to be a better way" — there is.

Start your 7-day free trial at adstack.io. Cancel anytime. Your spreadsheet will thank you.

P.S. — They just added a feature that auto-optimizes creative in real-time based on performance data. My last agency needed 3 weeks and a "strategy session" to do what AdStack does in 3 hours.
